prompts: A multimodal model processing both textual and visual inputs typically involves separate preprocessing pathways for each modality. Text is processed through embeddings, possibly followed by transformers or RNNs, while images are processed via convolutional neural networks (CNNs) or vision transformers to extract features. These processed features are then integrated in a joint representation space through techniques like concatenation, attention mechanisms, or a fusion layer. The integrated representation is finally passed through additional layers for downstream tasks such as classification, retrieval, or generation, allowing the model to leverage complementary information from both text and image inputs.
